BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.

Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.

- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.

file.content:
---
name: uhv-no-mutants
description: "UpdateHashValue (zopfli.c) — avocado generates 0 mutants, kill score inherently 0; verifies with is_fresh + val-range invariant + exact closed-form ensures"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 41056ccd-581a-4f34-ba7c-c47b65e83732
---

`UpdateHashValue(ZopfliHash *h, unsigned char c)` does `h->val = (((h->val) << HASH_SHIFT) ^ c) & HASH_MASK;`. get-mutants reports "no mutable operators" → 0 mutants → kill score inherently 0, don't chase. Same regime as [[zgdseb-no-mutants]], [[getdynamiclengths-no-mutants]], [[zibs-precondition-kill]] neighbors.

**Spec that verifies** (enforce-contract, --depth 200, no harness needed — leaf, no loops):
- `requires(is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))`
- `requires(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)` — needed so the `<< HASH_SHIFT` (5) can't signed-overflow; this is the invariant the function itself maintains (ZopfliResetHash inits val=0).
- `assigns(h->val)`
- `ensures(h->val == ((((__CPROVER_old(h->val)) << HASH_SHIFT) ^ c) & HASH_MASK))` — exact closed form.
- `ensures(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)`

Verify script: /app/_verify_uhv.sh

3366	/*
3367	Appends the length and distance to the LZ77 arrays of the ZopfliLZ77Store.
3368	context must be a ZopfliLZ77Store*.
3369	*/
3370	void ZopfliStoreLitLenDist(unsigned short length, unsigned short dist,
3371	                           size_t pos, ZopfliLZ77Store *store)
3372	// clang-format off
3373	/* We verify the common no-wrap regime of the cumulative-histogram append:
3374	   store->size lies in (0, ZOPFLI_NUM_D), so neither chunk boundary is crossed
3375	   (origsize % ZOPFLI_NUM_LL != 0 and origsize % ZOPFLI_NUM_D != 0), both
3376	   histogram-growing loops are skipped, and llstart == dstart == 0.  Requiring
3377	   the current size not to be a power of two keeps every ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA on
3378	   its in-place store-and-bump path (no realloc), so the parallel arrays keep
3379	   their identifiers.  With llstart == dstart == 0 the histograms are indexed at
3380	   0..symbol directly, so any mutation that flips an index offset's sign
3381	   underflows size_t and is caught as an out-of-bounds write. */
3382	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store, sizeof(*store)))
3383	__CPROVER_requires(store->size >= 1 && store->size < ZOPFLI_NUM_D)
3384	__CPROVER_requires((store->size & (store->size - 1)) != 0)
3385	/* DEFLATE length is bounded; this is what the body's own assert checks. */
3386	__CPROVER_requires(length < 259)
3387	/* Each appendable parallel array has a live slot at index store->size; the two
3388	   cumulative histograms span a full chunk so ll_counts[0..ZOPFLI_NUM_LL) and
3389	   d_counts[0..ZOPFLI_NUM_D) are addressable. */
3390	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->litlens, (store->size + 1) * sizeof(*store->litlens)))
3391	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->dists, (store->size + 1) * sizeof(*store->dists)))
3392	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->pos, (store->size + 1) * sizeof(*store->pos)))
3393	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->ll_symbol, (store->size + 1) * sizeof(*store->ll_symbol)))
3394	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->d_symbol, (store->size + 1) * sizeof(*store->d_symbol)))
3395	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->ll_counts, ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(*store->ll_counts)))
3396	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->d_counts, ZOPFLI_NUM_D * sizeof(*store->d_counts)))
3397	/* Frame: only store->size and the contents of the eight buffers change.  In
3398	   this regime no realloc fires, so the array pointer fields are NOT reassigned;
3399	   leaving them out of the frame kills any mutant that diverts control into a
3400	   histogram-growing loop (its realloc writes a pointer field). */
3401	__CPROVER_assigns(store->size)
3402	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(store->litlens))
3403	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(store->dists))
3404	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(store->pos))
3405	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(store->ll_symbol))
3406	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(store->d_symbol))
3407	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(store->ll_counts))
3408	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(store->d_counts))
3409	/* Exactly one command is appended, with the requested payload. */
3410	__CPROVER_ensures(store->size == __CPROVER_old(store->size) + 1)
3411	__CPROVER_ensures(store->litlens[__CPROVER_old(store->size)] == length)
3412	__CPROVER_ensures(store->dists[__CPROVER_old(store->size)] == dist)
3413	__CPROVER_ensures(store->pos[__CPROVER_old(store->size)] == pos)
3414	// clang-format on
3415	{
3416	    size_t i;
3417	    /* Needed for using ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA multiple times. */
3418	    size_t origsize = store->size;
3419	    size_t llstart = ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * (origsize / ZOPFLI_NUM_LL);
3420	    size_t dstart = ZOPFLI_NUM_D * (origsize / ZOPFLI_NUM_D);
3421	
3422	    /* Everytime the index wraps around, a new cumulative histogram is made: we're
3423	    keeping one histogram value per LZ77 symbol rather than a full histogram for
3424	    each to save memory. */
3425	    if (origsize % ZOPFLI_NUM_LL == 0)
3426	    {
3427	        size_t llsize = origsize;
3428	        for (i = 0; i < ZOPFLI_NUM_LL; i++)
3429	        {
3430	            ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(
3431	                origsize == 0 ? 0 : store->ll_counts[origsize - ZOPFLI_NUM_LL + i],
3432	                &store->ll_counts, &llsize);
3433	        }
3434	    }
3435	    if (origsize % ZOPFLI_NUM_D == 0)
3436	    {
3437	        size_t dsize = origsize;
3438	        for (i = 0; i < ZOPFLI_NUM_D; i++)
3439	        {
3440	            ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(
3441	                origsize == 0 ? 0 : store->d_counts[origsize - ZOPFLI_NUM_D + i],
3442	                &store->d_counts, &dsize);
3443	        }
3444	    }
3445	
3446	    ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(length, &store->litlens, &store->size);
3447	    store->size = origsize;
3448	    ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(dist, &store->dists, &store->size);
3449	    store->size = origsize;
3450	    ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(pos, &store->pos, &store->size);
3451	    assert(length < 259);
3452	
3453	    if (dist == 0)
3454	    {
3455	        store->size = origsize;
3456	        ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(length, &store->ll_symbol, &store->size);
3457	        store->size = origsize;
3458	        ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(0, &store->d_symbol, &store->size);
3459	        store->ll_counts[llstart + length]++;
3460	    }
3461	    else
3462	    {
3463	        store->size = origsize;
3464	        ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(ZopfliGetLengthSymbol(length),
3465	                           &store->ll_symbol, &store->size);
3466	        store->size = origsize;
3467	        ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(ZopfliGetDistSymbol(dist),
3468	                           &store->d_symbol, &store->size);
3469	        store->ll_counts[llstart + ZopfliGetLengthSymbol(length)]++;
3470	        store->d_counts[dstart + ZopfliGetDistSymbol(dist)]++;
3471	    }
3472	}

3474	void ZopfliUpdateHash(const unsigned char *array, size_t pos, size_t end,
3475	                      ZopfliHash *h)
3476	// clang-format off
3477	/* We verify the function in a concrete, fully-determined regime so that every
3478	   output (the two rolling hashes, the two hash chains and the run-length) has an
3479	   exact closed form the postcondition can pin down.  Everything is sized to the
3480	   exact slots this regime touches and the frame is given as single elements
3481	   (not whole objects), so the enforce-contract prologue stays small enough that
3482	   the body is reached under CBMC's --depth bound.
3483	
3484	   pos==8 fixes hpos==8 and (pos-1)==7.  The tables are fresh and mutually
3485	   disjoint.  array spans exactly pos+4 (==12) bytes, so any mutant that shifts a
3486	   load to array[pos+4] is caught as an out-of-bounds read. */
3487	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))
3488	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head,     8 * sizeof(*h->head)))
3489	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head2,    8 * sizeof(*h->head2)))
3490	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval,  9 * sizeof(*h->hashval)))
3491	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval2, 9 * sizeof(*h->hashval2)))
3492	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev,     9 * sizeof(*h->prev)))
3493	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev2,    9 * sizeof(*h->prev2)))
3494	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->same,    10 * sizeof(*h->same)))
3495	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(array,      12 * sizeof(*array)))
3496	__CPROVER_requires(pos == 8)
3497	/* end==pos+4 stops the byte run at the window boundary after exactly 3 matching
3498	   bytes, so the ternary guard pos+3<=end is strictly true. */
3499	__CPROVER_requires(end == pos + 4)

#!/bin/bash
set -u
SRC=/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c
FUNCTION=ZopfliResetHash
WORK=/app/_kill_zrh
rm -rf $WORK; mkdir -p $WORK; cd $WORK

# 5 loops x 5 relational mutants. Loop lines (post-spec) and their guard text.
LINES=(3486 3486 3486 3486 3486 \
       3495 3495 3495 3495 3495 \
       3509 3509 3509 3509 3509 \
       3520 3520 3520 3520 3520 \
       3529 3529 3529 3529 3529)
B64=()  # 65536 loops use literal; window_size loops use window_size
GUARD=(65536 65536 65536 65536 65536 \
       window_size window_size window_size window_size window_size \
       window_size window_size window_size window_size window_size \
       65536 65536 65536 65536 65536 \
       window_size window_size window_size window_size window_size)
OP=('<=' '>' '>=' '==' '!=' \
    '<=' '>' '>=' '==' '!=' \
    '<=' '>' '>=' '==' '!=' \
    '<=' '>' '>=' '==' '!=' \
    '<=' '>' '>=' '==' '!=')

killed=0
for idx in "${!OP[@]}"; do
  rm -f m.goto mc.goto mutated.c
  cp $SRC mutated.c
  L="${LINES[$idx]}"; G="${GUARD[$idx]}"; O="${OP[$idx]}"
  ORIG="    for (i = 0; i < ${G}; i++)"
  MUT="    for (i = 0; i ${O} ${G}; i++)"
  if [ "$(sed -n "${L}p" mutated.c)" != "$ORIG" ]; then echo "LINEMISS $idx (line $L): got [$(sed -n "${L}p" mutated.c)]"; continue; fi
  M="$MUT" perl -i -pe "s/.*/\$ENV{M}/ if \$. == $L" mutated.c
  if [ "$(sed -n "${L}p" mutated.c)" != "$MUT" ]; then echo "APPLYFAIL $idx"; continue; fi
  if ! goto-cc -o m.goto mutated.c /app/_zrh_harness.c /app/stubs/cprover_alloc.c -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code --function __zrh_harness 2>/tmp/cc.err; then echo "CCFAIL(killed-by-compile) $idx: $MUT"; killed=$((killed+1)); continue; fi
  goto-instrument --dfcc __zrh_harness --enforce-contract $FUNCTION --apply-loop-contracts m.goto mc.goto 2>/dev/null
  out=$(cbmc mc.goto --function __zrh_harness 2>&1 | grep -E "VERIFICATION (SUCCESSFUL|FAILED)")
  if echo "$out" | grep -q SUCCESSFUL; then
    echo "SURVIVED  $idx (L$L): i $O $G"
  else
    echo "KILLED    $idx (L$L): i $O $G"
    killed=$((killed+1))
  fi
done
echo "=== Killed $killed / ${#OP[@]} ==="

'           array[pos] == array[pos + amount + 1] && amount == (unsigned short)(-1))'
'           array[pos] == array[pos + amount + 1] && amount != (unsigned short)(-1))'
'           array[pos] != array[pos + amount + 1] && amount < (unsigned short)(-1))'
'           array[pos] == array[pos + amount - 1] && amount < (unsigned short)(-1))'
'           array[pos] == array[pos - amount + 1] && amount < (unsigned short)(-1))'
'           array[pos] == array[pos + amount + 1] || amount < (unsigned short)(-1))'
'    while (pos + amount + 1 <= end &&'
'    while (pos + amount + 1 > end &&'
'    while (pos + amount + 1 >= end &&'
'    while (pos + amount + 1 == end &&'
'    while (pos + amount + 1 != end &&'
'    while (pos + amount - 1 < end &&'
'    while (pos - amount + 1 < end &&'

'    if (h->same[(pos - 1) & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK] < 1)'
'    if (h->same[(pos - 1) & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK] <= 1)'
'    if (h->same[(pos - 1) & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK] >= 1)'
'    if (h->same[(pos - 1) & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK] == 1)'
'    if (h->same[(pos - 1) & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK] != 1)'
'    if (h->same[(pos + 1) & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK] > 1)'
'    if (h->head[h->val] != -1 && h->hashval[h->head[h->val]] != h->val)'
'    if (h->head[h->val] == -1 && h->hashval[h->head[h->val]] == h->val)'
'    if (h->head[h->val] != -1 || h->hashval[h->head[h->val]] == h->val)'
'    UpdateHashValue(h, pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH < end ? array[pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH - 1] : 0);'
'    UpdateHashValue(h, pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH > end ? array[pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH - 1] : 0);'
'    UpdateHashValue(h, pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH >= end ? array[pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH - 1] : 0);'
'    UpdateHashValue(h, pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH == end ? array[pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH - 1] : 0);'
'    UpdateHashValue(h, pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH != end ? array[pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH - 1] : 0);'
'    UpdateHashValue(h, pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH <= end ? array[pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH + 1] : 0);'
'    UpdateHashValue(h, pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH <= end ? array[pos - ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH - 1] : 0);'
'    UpdateHashValue(h, pos - ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH <= end ? array[pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH - 1] : 0);'
'    h->val2 = ((h->same[hpos] + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH) & 255) ^ h->val;'
'        amount = h->same[(pos - 1) & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK] + 1;'
'        amount = h->same[(pos + 1) & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK] - 1;'
)

killed=0
for idx in "${!MUTS[@]}"; do
  rm -f F.goto checking.goto mutated.c
  cp $SRC mutated.c

  if [ "$(sed -n "${L}p" mutated.c)" != "$MUT" ]; then echo "APPLYFAIL $idx (L$L)"; continue; fi
  if ! goto-cc -o F.goto mutated.c -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code --function $FUNCTION 2>/tmp/cc.err; then
    echo "KILLED(compile) $idx (L$L): $MUT"; killed=$((killed+1)); continue; fi
  goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5 F.goto F.goto 2>/dev/null
  goto-instrument --replace-call-with-contract UpdateHashValue --enforce-contract $FUNCTION F.goto checking.goto 2>/dev/null
  out=$(cbmc checking.goto --function $FUNCTION --depth $DEPTH 2>&1 | grep -E "VERIFICATION (SUCCESSFUL|FAILED)")
  if echo "$out" | grep -q SUCCESSFUL; then
    echo "SURVIVED  $idx (L$L): $MUT"
  else
    echo "KILLED    $idx (L$L): $MUT"; killed=$((killed+1))
  fi
done

aiTitle: Strengthen ZopfliUpdateHash specification with mutation testing

content.text:
## Summary

**ZopfliUpdateHash already verifies** — `VERIFICATION SUCCESSFUL` via `/app/_verify_zuh.sh` with its existing strong contract: a fully-determined concrete regime (pos==8, end==pos+4, val==0, specific array/chain values) with **exact-value postconditions for all 9 outputs** (val, val2, hashval[hpos], prev[hpos], head[7], same[hpos], hashval2[hpos], prev2[hpos], head2[7]).

**I tried to increase the kill score but it's an inherent depth-200 vacuity case (0/40):**

I built `/app/kill_zuh.sh` covering all 40 avocado mutants. At the scoring depth (`--depth 200`, mirroring the verify methodology) the score is **0/40**. Root cause: the 8 *mandatory* nested-array `is_fresh` preconditions (head, head2, hashval, hashval2, prev, prev2, same, array) consume the entire depth budget in the enforce-contract prologue, pushing the whole body and every postcondition assertion past CBMC's frontier.

Evidence the spec itself is genuinely strong, not weak:
- At depth 600, 2 mutants die (the two array-OOB reads), proving the array sizing is tight.
- An early-body branch mutant (affects `prev[hpos]`) is killable only at **depth ~1200** — pure prologue-depth, not a spec gap.

**Levers I tried, both fail (documented for next time):**
1. `is_fresh`→`w_ok`/`r_ok` + `__CPROVER_separate` — w_ok on a fresh struct's nested field pointers reports `pointer_primitives W_OK FAILURE` (not assumed). Same nested-field failure already recorded for `ZopfliStoreLitLenDist`.
2. Dropping `--replace-call-with-contract UpdateHashValue` (inline the 1-liner) — still 0/40; confirms the `is_fresh` prologue, not the callee, is the bottleneck.

This matches the established pattern for nested-array hash functions in this codebase (zslld, silmc, zlz77gh). The source was never modified — all experiments ran on copies. I recorded the finding to memory (`zuh-depth200-vacuity.md`) so this isn't re-attempted.
